_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize AK House batch (districts 34-38): Tomaszewski, Carrick, Schwanke, Edgmon, Jimmie. Two candidacy catches: Tomaszewski + Edgmon are NOT running for their House seats in 2026 (both filed for State Senate) - sitting records preserved, candidacy_status updated to running_higher_office. Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cited, null where no evidence, no inference from party. Sparse-data discipline: declined to score a misleading 100% for Jimmie off one soft cell.

set.office: State Representative, House District 34 (sitting · 2026 candidate for State Senate District Q)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Evidence-finalized from cited record (akleg HB107 cosponsorship, Alaska Beacon 2024-10-29 forum/voting writeup, SB140 veto-sustain coverage). CANDIDACY: did NOT file for HD34 reelection; filed 2026-06-01 for State Senate District Q after Sen. Robb Myers retired; his wife Harmony filed for the vacated HD34 seat. Sitting HD34 record retained and scored. Consistent Christian-conservative record on life/marriage; thin on guns, elections, sovereignty, immigration (null, not inferred).

frank-tomaszewski.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. R, HD34 (Fairbanks), all-Republican House Minority caucus (34th Leg.). Documented Christian-conservative: cosponsor HB107 fetal-personhood; voted to ban transgender girls from girls' sports; voted against SOGI nondiscrimination bill; voted to sustain Dunleavy's SB140 veto; backs a full/statutory PFD. NOT running for HD34 in 2026 — filed for State Senate District Q (2026-06-01); wife Harmony Tomaszewski filed for HD34.

0.note: Cosponsor of HB107 (33rd Leg.), a fetal-personhood bill defining person/life from conception.

1.note: Cosponsoring a constitutional-personhood bill (vs merely a restriction) is advocacy for abolition.

2.note: No evidence on embryonic stem cell / IVF discard / chimeric research.

3.note: No evidence on euthanasia / physician-assisted suicide.

4.note: No documented abortion-industry PAC funding either way; credit cell held to high bar, left null.

0.note: No direct one-man-one-woman marriage statement located.

1.note: No evidence on same-sex marriage / civil unions in law.

2.note: Voted to ban transgender girls from girls' sports teams (Alaska Beacon, 2024-10-29).

3.note: No evidence on no-fault divorce reform.

4.note: Voted AGAINST advancing a bill barring discrimination based on gender identity/sexual orientation.

0.note: Sustained the SB140 veto, but that is not clean evidence of school-choice/homeschool support; left null.

1.note: No evidence on parental consent for minors' medical/gender interventions.

4.note: No evidence on faith-based adoption agencies.

0.note: Profiled as governing from evangelical principles, but no specific public-profession vote/statement to score.

2.note: Backs a full PFD but no balanced-budget-amendment position; left null.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Evidence-finalized from cited record (Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates 'Repro Champ' endorsement, HB17 birth-control sponsorship, HB164 secure-storage sponsorship + red-flag advocacy). Consistent progressive Democrat; thin/absent on sovereignty, immigration, religious liberty, elections (null, not inferred). Confirmed running 2026; House Majority Coalition.

ashley-carrick.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. D, HD35 (Fairbanks), bipartisan House Majority Coalition; first openly bisexual AK legislator. Documented progressive: PP 'Repro Champ' endorsement, sponsored HB17 (birth-control access), introduced HB164 (mandatory firearm secure-storage) and advanced red-flag legislation as State Affairs chair. Confirmed running 2026.

0.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood as a 'Repro Champ'; pro-choice, does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-reproductive-rights record (sponsored HB17); opposes, not advocates, abortion abolition.

4.note: Accepted Planned Parenthood Action Fund electoral endorsement/backing.

0.note: First openly bisexual AK legislator and LGBTQ advocate; does not affirm marriage as one-man-one-woman.

2.note: No specific transgender-ideology vote/statement located; left null rather than inferred.

4.note: LGBTQ advocate; supports promotion/protection of LGBTQ identity in policy.

0.note: Public-school-funding champion (cosponsored BSA increase) but no direct anti-school-choice vote; left null.

2.note: Cites fiscal sustainability generally; no balanced-budget-amendment position.

0.note: No paper-ballot position located (RCV-retention is party-inference only).
Criminal-Justice fellowship cohort but no pro-police/defunding position to score.
Gun-control sponsor, but no explicit permitless-carry statement; left null.

1.note: Introduced HB164 (mandatory secure storage) and advanced red-flag/extreme-risk legislation.

2.note: No explicit NFA/GCA repeal position located.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Evidence-finalized from cited record (Juneau Empire 2025-04 trans-care budget amendment, webcenterfairbanks 2024-10-30 HB183 statement, ADN/Alaska Public education-veto votes, Alaska Beacon voter guide on RCV, Freedom Index). First-term R, all-Republican House Minority caucus. Conservative on marriage/sex and state sovereignty; life cells left null (Medicaid-funding vote is a restriction, not a personhood/abolition affirmation). Listed incumbent for 2026; no withdrawal found.

rebecca-schwanke.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. R, HD36 (Interior AK — Glennallen/Delta), all-Republican House Minority caucus; first elected 2024, 6 yrs school board. Documented conservative: voted (party-line) for budget amendment restricting state-funded transgender care, named HB183 (biological-sex school sports) a bill she wished had passed, voted against the HB57 BSA increase / to sustain Dunleavy's education vetoes, supports ranked-choice-voting repeal, names federal overreach (land/wildlife) a top obstacle. Freedom Index 60%.

0.note: No named personhood vote; the antiabortion Medicaid-language vote is a funding restriction, not a personhood affirmation. Left null to hold the bar.

1.note: Medicaid-funding restriction is not abortion abolition; left null.

4.note: No documented PAC funding either way; left null.

2.note: Voted (R caucus, party-line 21-19 w/ only Rs Stutes & Kopp crossing) for budget amendment restricting state-funded transgender care; named HB183 (biological-sex sports) a bill she wished had passed.

4.note: Trans-care funding restriction + HB183 biological-sex sports support = opposes LGBTQ-ideology promotion in policy/schools.

0.note: Voted against BSA increase / to sustain education vetoes (fiscal restraint), but no direct school-choice/homeschool position; left null.

2.note: Fiscal restraint (opposes pensions, BSA increase) but no balanced-budget-amendment position; Freedom Index is an aggregate, not these cells. Left null.

0.note: Supports RCV repeal, but no specific paper-ballot position; RCV does not map to a rubric cell. Left null.
Listed public safety as a funding priority — too generic for the affirmative-advocacy bar; left null.

2.note: No specific guns/life/education federal-resistance vote located.

3.note: Names federal overreach (wildlife/land management) a top obstacle and affirms state authority/subsistence priority — affirms state sovereignty against federal action (statement, soft).

4.note: No specific federal-land/elections-capture vote located; left null.

set.office: State Representative — Speaker of the House (sitting · 2026 candidate for State Senate)

profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-17 — Evidence-finalized from cited record (HB99 LGBTQ-nondiscrimination cosponsorship, ADN PFD/budget coverage, Ballot Measure 2 support, Bristol Bay Forever Act). CANDIDACY: did NOT file for HD37 reelection; filed 2026-05-27 for the open State Senate seat (Sen. Hoffman retiring). Still House Speaker. Center-left Independent leading a 14D/5I/2R coalition; conservative-aligned positions thin/absent. Record is thin against this rubric — only two cells documented (both LGBTQ-related); most cells null, not inferred.

bryce-edgmon.notes_set: Evidence-state finalization 2026-06-17. Independent (formerly D), HD37 (Dillingham/Bristol Bay/SW AK), Speaker of the Alaska House, first Alaska Native Speaker; leads a 21-member bipartisan majority (14 D / 5 I / 2 R). Documented center-left: cosponsor HB99 (LGBTQ nondiscrimination), backed smaller PFD to balance budget, supported 2020 Ballot Measure 2 (top-four open primaries + RCV), cosponsor Bristol Bay Forever Act (anti-Pebble). NOT running for HD37 in 2026 — filed 2026-05-27 for the open State Senate seat (Hoffman retiring); still Speaker.

0.note: No direct abortion/personhood position located (HB17 is contraception).

4.note: No documented PAC funding either way; left null.

2.note: Cosponsor of HB99 adding gender identity to AK anti-discrimination law — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Cosponsor of HB99 (LGBTQ nondiscrimination) — supports LGBTQ identity protections in policy.

0.note: Public-school-funding leader (HB57 override) but no direct anti-school-choice vote; left null.

2.note: Backed a smaller PFD to balance the budget, but no balanced-budget-amendment position; left null.

0.note: Supported Ballot Measure 2 (open primaries + RCV); does not map to a rubric cell. Left null.

2.note: No federal-overreach-resistance vote located (anti-Pebble fisheries protection is environmental, not anti-federal).

No documented positions on the rubric's life/marriage/family/liberty/economic/election/immigration/self-defense questions; campaign emphasis is rural public safety (VPSO/MMIP), subsistence/hunting-fishing rights, and rural cost-of-living. Freedom Index 0% (2025-26). Confirmed running 2026 (rematch vs CJ McCormick). All cells null — thin first-term record, nothing inferred or fabricated.
